title: Intel's IA Continuum Strategy Anticipates a Much Broader Computing Market
author: Peter S. Kastner
abstract: Aberdeen Group white paper analyzing Intel's IA Continuum strategy for the 2010s decade. The paper examines how Intel planned to expand its IA architecture from traditional PCs into smartphones, consumer electronics, embedded systems-on-a-chip, datacenter/HPC, and emerging markets. Covers Atom, Core, Xeon product lines, Moblin/MeeGo software strategy, Larrabee and Terascale R&D, and market challenges including ARM competition in mobile.
importance_rationale: Comprehensive analysis of Intel's decade-defining IA Continuum strategy; captures Intel's pivot from PC-centric to computing-continuum company at a critical inflection point.
relevance_rationale: Intel's IA architecture dominance in servers/datacenter proved lasting; the continuum concept anticipated IoT and edge computing trends still unfolding.
prescience_rationale: Server/datacenter predictions proved highly accurate; Atom mobile/smartphone predictions largely failed as ARM dominated mobile; Larrabee was cancelled but GPU computing concept validated.
